rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 20, 2026, line 1. PETITIONER MICHELLE HARRISON'S Motion to Compel Arbitration. Hearing required. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 20, 2026, line 2. PETITIONER MIA SHEW-SMITH'S Motion to Compel Arbitration. This petition is the same as the lead petition in all material respects and the court adopts its reasoning and analysis from the lead case. Petitioner as the prevailing party is directed to adopt the Harrison order to this case and serve it on opposing counsel pursuant to Rule of Court 3.1312 before submitting it to the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN FREDIANI VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Order To File Records Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 20, 2026, line 30. PLAINTIFFS STEVEN FREDIANI, ROBERT RIGLEY, RICHARD TROCHE, MICHAEL DION'S Application For Order To File Records Under Seal. The motion to seal is granted. Because the parties settled, the records at issue were not submitted to the court as a basis for adjudication and no further findings are required. rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY VAESAU VS. ANTWON CHILDES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Serve Summons Within Two Years
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 20, 2026, line 32. DEFENDANTS ANTWON CHILDES, SHARON LYLES' Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Serve Summons Within Two Years. The unopposed motion to dismiss this action for delay in prosecution is granted. Plaintiff has not yet served the summons and has not appeared on orders to show cause. "The unexcused failure to serve a summons within the two-year period prima facie constitutes a sufficient ground for dismissal. In such a case there is no requirement for an affirmative showing of prejudice." (Scarzella v. DeMers (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 1762, 1768-1769 [citation simplified and footnote omitted].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: A. S. VS. MARK ALAN ILLSLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Non-Resident Plaintiff To File An Undertaking; Memo Of P&As; Declaration Of Erin Hallissy
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 20, 2026, line 33. DEFENDANT MARK ILLSLEY'S Motion For Order Requiring Non-Resident Plaintiff To File An Undertaking. Defendant Mark Alan Illsley's motion to require out-of-state resident plaintiff A.S. to post security is denied. Illsley seeks relief under Code of Civil Procedure, section 1030. That statute provides that a defendant may seek an order requiring a nonresident plaintiff "to file an undertaking to secure an award of costs and attorney's fees which may be awarded in the action or special proceeding." (Id., subd. (a).) The moving defendant must show that "there is a reasonable possibility that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in the action." (Id., subd. (b).) If the court finds the grounds for the motion set out in subdivision (b) are established, "the court shall order that the plaintiff file the undertaking in an amount specified in the court's order as security for costs and attorney's fees." (Id., subd. (c).) The court concludes that it is Illsley's burden to show that the grounds for the motion have been established, i.e. that (among other things) there is a reasonable possibility that he will obtain judgment. Illsley's motion describes conflicting deposition testimony and some of the defenses that he will seek to establish at trial but does not make an evidentiary showing of substantial evidence from which the jury could render a defense verdict. Illsley's motion also asks the court to treat any recovery by A.S. that is less than his section 998 offer as a defense verdict. But the statute requires Illsley to show that he will obtain a judgment in his favor, not be subject to a plaintiff's judgment more favorable than his offer. Because Illsley has failed to sustain his burden on this motion, it is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. RJ BANKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 20, 2026, line 35. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Defendant R.J. Banks failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the requests is deemed admitted by Defendant. (Id., sec. 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).) Plaintiff does not seek sanctions, but they are mandatory in this instance. (Id., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) The court orders Defendant to pay $50 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
